text: this killed me 💀💀💀 chapter 236 was peak
I'm crying actual tears at gojo's flashback. why is this so well written
I am dead. that panel layout. that final smile. i need a moment
kashimo really popped out for one chapter then died like that 💀
todo's haircut in s2 ep 1 💀💀💀
Gege you genius bastard, that final chapter was something else. 10/10 ending
I hate this man Gege Akutami. the way he sets up payoffs and then refuses to deliver them. brilliant. terrible. perfect.
fuck Gege for that last page. genuinely. the foreshadowing was insane in retrospect.
bring back gojo gege 🙏🙏🙏 it's been two years man
🙏 please let nobara come back. just one panel. one acknowledgment. anything
PEAK
tragic.
actually insane chapter
this man is unhinged i love him
Sukuna's writing post-Heian flashback is genuinely some of the best villain work I've read. the philosophy actually tracks.
Higuruma had the most coherent character arc in the entire culling game. underrated guy.
yuta is so well constructed as a foil to itadori, both in volume 0 and now. the parallels are intentional
Megumi as a protagonist falls flat. he's been passive for 200+ chapters and Gege still hasn't given him interiority
Maki post-massacre is a flat power-fantasy character. she had so much more depth before
why did Gege kill Gojo. this ruined the pacing of the final arc completely.
the culling game went on for too long and didn't pay off most of the new characters
the final fight resolution felt rushed. Yuji's last attack came out of nowhere
shibuya arc is one of the best paced shounen arcs ever written. every beat lands.
the hidden inventory flashback recontextualizes everything. it's the heart of the whole series tbh
MAPPA's animation in the gojo vs jogo fight is some of the best sakuga of the decade
the s2 production schedule was visibly killing the animators. studio needs reform
anime-onlys are getting filtered. the manga panels were so much more impactful
love Sukuna as a written character, but I hate what he did to Megumi. this isn't the redemption arc I wanted
MAPPA's animation in s2 was honestly carrying a script that didn't always deserve it. the rooftop scene though, that frame composition
the art is incredible. the dialogue is sometimes janky. taken as a whole I love it but I won't pretend it's perfect
what chapter does the shibuya arc start? trying to plan a reread
where can I read JJK 0 online? not finding it on the usual sites
is the manga finished or still ongoing? coming back after a long break
Megumi is alright I guess. not my favorite but I get why people like him
I dropped this around chapter 200. might pick it back up someday
oh great another flashback chapter. just what we needed at this point in the arc
wow another chapter where megumi gets sidelined. groundbreaking storytelling really.
this fight is so unfair I'm losing my mind. why is sukuna like this. WHY IS HE LIKE THIS
Gojo absolutely ruined Sukuna in this chapter and it was beautiful to read
love this series so much. it's the only manga that's made me feel anything in years
the panel where gojo says nah id win is one of the most iconic moments in modern shounen
the ending was deeply unsatisfying. years of buildup for what amounts to a shrug. I'm out.
Gege has no idea how to write female characters. it's been a problem since vol 0 and only gotten worse
Gojo vs Sukuna was the fight of the decade. both characters got to show what makes them great.
Yuta and Yuji parallels in the final arc are doing a lot of heavy lifting that the writing isn't earning imo
domain expansion: malevolent shrine 🔪🔪🔪
throughout heaven and earth, I alone am the honored one
anime onlys complaining about pacing when manga readers waited weeks between chapters of this same scene
what if Yuta was supposed to be the protagonist all along and Itadori was just a red herring
calling it now: there's going to be a sequel that recontextualizes the ending
I'm actually not okay after that chapter. need to step away from social media for a while
rereading the goodbyes scene and I just. man. why does Gege do this to us
if I liked JJK what should I read next? finished the manga and need a fix
I genuinely think Kenjaku's plan was the most coherent villain plan in modern shounen, even if the execution lost steam
Mahoraga adaptation is a literal metaphor for shounen power creep. Gege knows what he's doing
